ddls:
CREATE TABLE "patients" (
  "patient_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each patient */,
  "patient_name" TEXT /* Full name of the patient */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the patient */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the patient */,
  "medical_history" TEXT /* Medical history of the patient */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the patient */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the patient */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the patient */,
  "emergency_contact" TEXT /* Emergency contact information for the patient */,
  "insurance_provider" TEXT /* Insurance provider of the patient */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("patient_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "tests" (
  "test_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test */,
  "test_name" TEXT /* Name of the test (e.g., Urinalysis, Blood Chemistry) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the test */,
  "test_type" TEXT /* Type of test (e.g., urinalysis, blood chemistry) */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* ID of the department the test belongs to */,
  "frequency" TEXT /* Frequency of the test */,
  "cost" REAL /* Cost of the test */,
  "preparation_instructions" TEXT /* Preparation instructions for the test */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("test_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tests_department_id FOREIGN KEY ("department_id") REFERENCES departments ("department_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tests_test_type FOREIGN KEY ("test_type") REFERENCES test_types ("type_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "test_results" (
  "result_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test result */,
  "patient_id" INTEGER /* ID of the patient who took the test */,
  "test_id" INTEGER /* ID of the test */,
  "result_date" TEXT /* Date the test result was recorded */,
  "gravity" REAL /* Gravity of the test result */,
  "ph" REAL /* pH level of the test result */,
  "osmo" REAL /* Osmolality of the test result */,
  "cond" REAL /* Conductivity of the test result */,
  "urea" REAL /* Urea level of the test result */,
  "calc" REAL /* Calcium level of the test result */,
  "doctor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the doctor who interpreted the test result */,
  "interpretation" TEXT /* Interpretation of the test result */,
  "reference_range" TEXT /* Reference range for the test results */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the test result */,
  "version" INTEGER /* Version of the test result */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("result_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_results_test_id FOREIGN KEY ("test_id") REFERENCES tests ("test_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_results_doctor_id FOREIGN KEY ("doctor_id") REFERENCES doctors ("doctor_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_results_patient_id FOREIGN KEY ("patient_id") REFERENCES patients ("patient_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "test_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the test type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the test type */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* ID of the department the test type belongs to */,
  "frequency" TEXT /* Frequency of the test type */,
  "average_cost" REAL /* Average cost of the test type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_types_department_id FOREIGN KEY ("department_id") REFERENCES departments ("department_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "departments" (
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each department */,
  "department_name" TEXT /* Name of the department */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the department */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the department */,
  "contact_information" TEXT /* Contact information for the department */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("department_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "doctors" (
  "doctor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each doctor */,
  "doctor_name" TEXT /* Full name of the doctor */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the doctor */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* ID of the department the doctor belongs to */,
  "specialty" TEXT /* Specialty of the doctor */,
  "license_number" TEXT /* Medical license number of the doctor */,
  "availability" TEXT /* Availability schedule of the doctor */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("doctor_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_doctors_department_id FOREIGN KEY ("department_id") REFERENCES departments ("department_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "appointments" (
  "appointment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each appointment */,
  "patient_id" INTEGER /* ID of the patient */,
  "doctor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the doctor */,
  "appointment_date" TEXT /* Date of the appointment */,
  "start_time" TEXT /* Start time of the appointment */,
  "end_time" TEXT /* End time of the appointment */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the appointment (e.g., scheduled, completed, canceled) */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the appointment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("appointment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_appointments_patient_id FOREIGN KEY ("patient_id") REFERENCES patients ("patient_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_appointments_doctor_id FOREIGN KEY ("doctor_id") REFERENCES doctors ("doctor_id")
)
column_names: Date of birth of the patient
